name: Vena FP&A Platform API
what_it_does: Vena Solutions REST API for Excel-native FP&A and corporate performance management platform. Enables AI agents to import and export financial data from Vena's Excel-based planning models, retrieve budgets and forecasts, access workflow status for planning cycles, manage template data imports, integrate Vena data with ERP and BI systems, and automate financial planning data flows.
use_cases: Importing ERP actuals into Vena planning templates from financial close agents
Retrieving consolidated budget and forecast data from FP&A reporting agents
Monitoring planning workflow status and approvals from process management agents
Syncing Vena financial data with BI and analytics platforms from reporting agents
not_for: Organizations without Excel-familiar finance teams (Vena's UX is Excel-native)
Large enterprise requiring multidimensional OLAP at scale without Excel workflow
Real-time financial analytics without batch-oriented planning cycles
best_when: A mid-market finance team that uses Excel workflows wants AI agents to automate data imports, maintain planning cycle status, and export financial outputs without rebuilding the Excel-based process.
avoid_when: FINANCIAL RISK: Budget and forecast data drives planning decisions. Excel-based models can have complex dependencies — validate data imports carefully to avoid formula errors in downstream calculations.

auth.notes: Vena uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. Developer portal for integration access. Service account credentials per deployment. API access included with enterprise subscriptions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise FP&A pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise annual contracts based on users and modules
pricing.notes: Canadian company (Toronto). Excel-native FP&A platform positioned for Excel-fluent finance teams. Mid-market to enterprise focus. Competes with Prophix and Adaptive Insights.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API client; documented with enterprise access

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Excel-based model complexity — data imports must not break formula dependencies
No public MCP server — REST API with OAuth2 or API key
No webhooks — requires polling or scheduled jobs for data pipeline
Excel-native architecture means data model differs from traditional CPM systems
Canadian company — CA and US data residency options
Planning cycle templates must exist before data imports — template management required
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: Budget and financial planning data. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2 and API key. CA/US data residency. Canadian company.

security_score_components.security_notes: Financial planning data. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2 and API key. CA/US data residency.
